title: OAuth Mobile Authentication Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of OAuth 2.0 PKCE flow, social login, and secure token storage in React Native.

questions.question: What does 'PKCE' (Proof Key for Code Exchange) add to the standard OAuth Authorization Code flow?

options.text: It encrypts the user's password before sending to the OAuth provider
It prevents malicious apps from intercepting the authorization code by binding the code exchange to a cryptographic secret generated by the legitimate app
It extends the expiry of access tokens from 1 hour to 24 hours
It allows the app to skip the browser redirect and authenticate directly

questions.question: After receiving a Google access token via OAuth in your mobile app, what is the correct next step?

options.text: Store the Google access token in SecureStore and use it directly for all API calls to your backend
Send the Google access token to YOUR backend, which verifies it with Google and issues your own JWT — never trust client-side OAuth tokens for authorization
Decode the JWT and read the user information directly from the token payload
Exchange the Google token for a Firebase token to enable database access

questions.question: Apple requires that any iOS app offering third-party social login must also include which feature?

options.text: Sign in with Face ID as a primary login option
Sign in with Apple — Apple's App Store guidelines reject apps with social login that do not also offer Sign in with Apple
Two-factor authentication via SMS
iCloud Keychain integration for password autofill

questions.question: Why should you use expo-secure-store instead of AsyncStorage for storing auth tokens?

options.text: SecureStore supports larger payload sizes than AsyncStorage
AsyncStorage is deprecated and no longer maintained
AsyncStorage stores data in plaintext; SecureStore uses iOS Keychain and Android Keystore (hardware-backed encryption) to protect sensitive credentials
AsyncStorage is synchronous and blocks the UI thread when reading tokens

questions.question: Access tokens typically expire in 1 hour. What mechanism prevents the user from being logged out every hour?

options.text: The OAuth provider automatically extends token expiry if the user is active
The app stores the user's password to re-authenticate automatically when the token expires
A long-lived refresh token is used to silently obtain a new access token without requiring the user to re-authenticate
Access tokens cannot expire in mobile apps — only web tokens expire
